description: Phase 1 — Read-only foundation (PLAN.md §9; ADR-0001 Tauri, ADR-0005 canvas graph, ADR-0010 SolidJS). Adds the Tauri v2 desktop shell + SolidJS/TS/Vite frontend and the read-only views: open repo, refs, virtualized commit list, commit-graph canvas, diff viewer, blame, file history, repository manager, command palette. EXIT: browse any repo's read views in a real window as well as Fork's. No mutations, no AI. Branches from main (which has Phase 0). Each story = one context-window commit. UI stories rely on build + typecheck + unit tests for loop verification; a manual window check is noted in progress.txt since Tauri windows cannot be verified headless in the loop.

userStories.title: Add Tauri v2 shell + SolidJS/TS/Vite frontend
userStories.description: As a user, I need a desktop window so Lady has a GUI.
acceptanceCriteria: Add src-tauri/ (Tauri v2 app crate) + ui/ (SolidJS + TypeScript + Vite) per ADR-0001/ADR-0010; the cargo workspace still builds
tauri.conf.json: app name 'Lady', 1200x800 window, frontendDist -> ui build output, devUrl -> vite dev server
`npm install` in ui/ then `cargo tauri build` succeeds and launches a window with a placeholder view
ui/node_modules, ui/dist, src-tauri/target stay git-ignored
cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/build clean; cargo deny check passes (Tauri deps are MIT/Apache); npm run build (tsc + vite) passes
userStories.notes: Prereqs verified present: node v22, npm 10.9, cargo-tauri. ADR-0001, ADR-0010.

userStories.title: Rust<->web command bridge (app_info)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the invoke bridge working so the frontend can call Rust.
acceptanceCriteria: #[tauri::command] app_info() -> { name, version } registered in src-tauri
ui invokes app_info on mount via @tauri-apps/api and renders the result
A TS type mirrors the app_info payload
App launches and shows app_info; cargo + npm run build green; clippy -D warnings clean
userStories.notes: Proves the command/event bridge before wiring real data.

userStories.title: open_repo + list_refs commands -> refs view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open a repo and see its branches, tags, and HEAD.
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri commands open_repo(path) -> RepoId and list_refs(repo) -> Vec<RefInfo> delegate to lady-git GixEngine
lady-proto types serialize across the bridge; TS types mirror RefInfo/RefKind
ui: a path input opens a repo and renders refs grouped (branches / tags / remotes / HEAD)
Works on the Lady repo; a unit test covers the command layer; cargo + npm run build green; cargo deny passes

userStories.title: walk_log command -> virtualized commit list
userStories.description: As a user, I want a scrollable list of commits that stays fast on huge repos.
acceptanceCriteria: Command walk_log(repo, { start, limit }) -> Vec<CommitMeta>, paged
ui renders a virtualized/windowed commit list (short oid + summary + author + relative time); only visible rows in the DOM
Incremental load on scroll; a large repo (e.g. this one or a synthetic one) scrolls without freezing
Command logic unit-tested; cargo + npm run build green; cargo deny passes
userStories.notes: Virtualization per PLAN.md §6 — never materialize whole history.

userStories.title: lady-graph crate: commit-graph lane layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I need lane assignment + edge routing so the graph can be drawn.
acceptanceCriteria: New crate crates/lady-graph (workspace member) depending on lady-proto
fn layout(commits: &[CommitMeta]) -> Vec<GraphRow { oid, lane, edges, refs }> assigns lanes and routes parent/child edges
Correct for linear history, branch, two-parent merge, and octopus merge; stable/deterministic given the same input
Unit tests (insta snapshots or explicit asserts) cover each case; cargo test/clippy -D warnings/fmt green; cargo deny passes
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §6 hard part — pure logic, no UI. Build incrementally; keep it streamable.

userStories.title: Commit-graph canvas render (hybrid)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the commit DAG drawn next to the commit list.
acceptanceCriteria: A data path feeds GraphRow to the frontend (command or extension of walk_log)
Canvas 2D draws lanes + edges; commit row text/refs render as aligned virtualized DOM (hybrid per ADR-0005)
devicePixelRatio handled (sharp on retina); theme color tokens passed into draw calls; canvas + DOM rows scroll in sync
Renders the Lady repo's graph in the window; npm run build + cargo build green; cargo deny passes
Manual window verification noted in progress.txt (cannot verify headless)
userStories.notes: ADR-0005 — signature view; render-target kept swappable behind the row-data interface.

userStories.title: lady-diff crate + diff command
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a diff engine so the UI can show changes.
acceptanceCriteria: New crate crates/lady-diff using imara-diff; produces FileDiff (hunks, lines, change kind) for a commit (or working file) vs its parent
Handles add / delete / modify / binary; image files flagged as image (no text diff)
Tauri command diff(repo, spec) -> FileDiff
Unit tests on the hunk model; cargo test/clippy/fmt green; cargo deny passes (imara-diff is Apache-2.0)
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §4 lady-diff. Partial-staging patch builder is NOT in scope here (Phase 2).

userStories.title: Diff viewer UI + syntax highlighting
userStories.description: As a user, I want to read diffs clearly with highlighting.
acceptanceCriteria: ui renders FileDiff with side-by-side AND unified modes (toggle)
Syntax highlighting for common languages (tree-sitter wasm or a JS highlighter); image diffs show both images
Added/removed/context lines styled; large diffs virtualized
npm run build + cargo build green; manual window verification noted in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 diff viewer (side-by-side, syntax, image diff).

userStories.title: Blame command + blame view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see who last changed each line.
acceptanceCriteria: Command blame(repo, path, at) -> Blame (per-line last commit) via lady-git/gix
ui blame view: each line shows a gutter with short oid + author + date
Unit test on the blame mapping; cargo + npm run build green; cargo deny passes

userStories.title: File history command + view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the commits that touched a file.
acceptanceCriteria: Command file_history(repo, path) -> Vec<CommitMeta> (commits affecting the path)
ui: pick a file -> list its history; click a commit -> show that commit's diff for the file (reuses the diff viewer)
Command logic unit-tested; cargo + npm run build green

userStories.title: Repository manager (open/clone/add, recent, tabs)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manage and switch between repositories.
acceptanceCriteria: ui repository manager: open existing, clone (shell-out `git clone` with progress per ADR-0003), add, and a recent-repos list
Multiple repos in tabs with custom groups; a star indicator on a tab when its repo has uncommitted changes (status check)
Recent repos + groups persisted to settings (TOML in the platform config dir via the directories crate)
cargo + npm run build green; cargo deny passes
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 repository manager. Clone uses shell-out git (ADR-0003).

userStories.title: Command palette (fuzzy quick launch)
userStories.description: As a user, I want a keyboard palette to jump to actions, branches, and files.
acceptanceCriteria: ui command palette opens on Cmd/Ctrl+P with fuzzy search (nucleo in Rust via a command, or a TS fuzzy lib) over actions, branches, and files
Selecting an entry runs the action or navigates to the branch/file; fully keyboard-driven
cargo + npm run build green
PHASE 1 EXIT: the window browses a repo's graph, diffs, blame, history, and repos as well as Fork's read views — note this in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 Quick Launch / command palette. After this PRD, the next is a Phase 2 write-ops PRD (new branchName ralph/phase-2-write-ops).
